lesson.menu_desc: Make an appointment and explain your medical history with used to -- I used to smoke, but I quit -- 28 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 16 -- At the Doctor's Office -- making an appointment and explaining your medical history
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Rubens Tofolo | Aula 16 -- At the Doctor's Office

listenings.text: Good morning, this is the front desk at the Riverside Medical Center calling for Mr. Rubens Tofolo. I am calling to confirm your appointment with Doctor Lima for a general checkup. Your appointment is on Thursday at ten o'clock in the morning. Please arrive fifteen minutes early so the receptionist can update your medical history and check your insurance. The doctor will examine you and may check your blood pressure. If you take any regular medicine, please bring the list with you. If you need to reschedule, call this number before Wednesday. We look forward to seeing you. Thank you and have a good day.

When a new patient comes to my office, the first thing I do is take a careful medical history. I ask about the past, because the past explains a lot. I ask, did you use to smoke? Did you use to take any medicine for a long time? Many patients used to have a health problem that is important for me to know. For example, one patient used to play professional sports, so his heart was very strong. Another patient used to skip breakfast every day for years. The medical history is not just about today. It is about the habits a person used to have, the treatment they used to follow, and the condition they used to live with. Good doctors listen to the whole story.

extra_audio.text: Here is how I book a checkup at a new clinic. First, I call the front desk and ask for an appointment. Then I give my name and explain why I need to see the doctor. After that, the receptionist offers me a day and a time. Next, I confirm the appointment and ask what documents to bring. Finally, I arrive early so they can update my medical history.
